side_compiler.note: Must use GOTOOLCHAIN=local to prevent toolchain directive override

profiling.wal_finding: WAL dominated by syscalls (36.8%) and GC (19.3%) - limited codegen opportunity

optimization_backlog.name: inline-items-find
optimization_backlog.desc: items.find cost 119 (budget 80). sort.Search inlined (cost 56) + closure (cost 39) + overhead (24). Reducing closure cost for closures passed to inlined funcs could help.
optimization_backlog.expected_impact: 2-5% on Get/Compact benchmarks (eliminates function call in btree binary search)
optimization_backlog.risk: medium - needs careful cost model change

optimization_backlog.name: bce-walk-alias-analysis
optimization_backlog.desc: generation.walk g.revs[l-i-1] bounds check NOT eliminable by prove pass because closure f could modify g.revs. Would require proving closure doesn't escape/modify receiver. Fundamental alias analysis limitation.
optimization_backlog.risk: high - requires deep alias analysis changes

optimization_backlog.name: inline-findGeneration
optimization_backlog.desc: (*keyIndex).findGeneration cost 81 (budget 80) - just 1 over! Called from hot get() path. But budget raise 80->85 showed no measurable improvement in prior attempt.
optimization_backlog.expected_impact: 0-1% (prior attempt at budget 85 showed no gain)

optimization_backlog.name: prove-strict-subtraction
optimization_backlog.desc: Uncomment 'if yLim.min > 0 { ft.update(v, x, lt) }' in detectSubRelations. Adds fact v < x when subtracting strictly positive value. Enables more BCE in certain patterns but didn't help walk() due to alias issue.
optimization_backlog.expected_impact: 0-1% (helps other patterns, not the main hot path)

optimization_backlog.name: escape-keyindex-put
optimization_backlog.desc: keyIndex.put: append patterns escape to heap. GC at ~19% of WAL CPU.

attempt_log.name: raise-inline-budget-85
attempt_log.lesson: Budget increase too small. findGeneration (cost 81) inlined but no measurable impact.

attempt_log.name: bce-walk-reverse-iteration
attempt_log.desc: Attempted to prove g.revs[l-i-1] bounds safe by uncommenting strict subtraction fact in detectSubRelations
attempt_log.detail: Root cause: g.revs is reloaded each iteration because closure f could modify it. The prove pass correctly cannot eliminate the check without alias analysis proving f doesn't modify g.revs. SliceLen from range init != SliceLen for bounds check.
attempt_log.lesson: BCE for reverse iteration through closure-calling loops requires alias analysis beyond current prove pass capabilities.

task_substitutions: Tasks 1,2,8 selected. Task 1 rebuilt side compiler (ephemeral /tmp). Task 2 substituted with Task 4 (profiling data still fresh from prior run). Task 4: investigated BCE for walk() reverse iteration - found alias analysis limitation blocking the optimization. Updated backlog priorities.
